summary: Animation audit across 40+ active scripts. Most scene transitions use PixelTransition autoload (good). Critical gaps: HUD life bar instant-updates, card panel naked visible toggles, end screen elements appear instantly with no stagger.
strengths: PixelTransition autoload is the standard scene change mechanism — good
ui_overlay_effects.gd has proper life delta flash + zoom tween (lines 158-183)
ui_overlay_endscreen.gd has forest tween + overlay fade (lines 21-41)
IntroCeltOS.gd has full stagger tween sequence
TransitionBiome.gd is the most tween-heavy file (17 usages)

findings.issue: _life_bar.value = current — instant update, no smooth tween
_life_bar.modulate = CRT_PALETTE[danger] — instant color snap, no tween
_card_panel.visible = true/false — naked toggle, no fade-in/out tween
_zone_label.visible = true/false — naked toggle, no fade tween
End screen elements (title, text, score, rewards, buttons) have no stagger reveal — all appear instantly after overlay fade
options_container.visible = true — options appear without stagger animation
get_tree().change_scene_to_file() called directly (bypasses PixelTransition) in fallback path — already tracked as E2E-BROC-FOREST-FALLBACK-TRANSITION
_enter_btn.visible = true instant before fade tween starts — acceptable (alpha=0 so invisible)
